definition: nearness in distance or time
example: We chose this apartment because of its proximity to the school—it's only a two-minute walk.

definition: showing careful judgment to avoid risks
example: It was prudent to bring an umbrella since the sky looked dark and cloudy.

definition: a period of isolation to prevent the spread of disease
example: The sick sailor was put in quarantine for two weeks, so he wouldn't infect the crew.

definition: the least number of members needed before a group can make official decisions
example: The club couldn't vote because only eight members showed up, and the quorum required twelve.

definition: to officially approve and make something valid
example: Three-quarters of the states must ratify an amendment before it becomes part of the Constitution.

definition: a fixed amount of something given to each person when supplies are limited
example: During the war each family received a weekly ration of sugar, butter, and meat.

definition: a break from work or school, or a hidden alcove
example: The students raced outside for recess and immediately started a game of kickball.

definition: to restore a friendly bond after a fight
example: The two best friends decided to reconcile after a week of not speaking to each other.

definition: a vote where all citizens decide on a single important question
example: Citizens voted in a referendum to decide whether the town should build a new sports stadium.

definition: to pay someone back money they have spent
example: The company will reimburse you for any supplies you buy for the school project.

definition: a period of great cultural artistic rebirth, especially in 14th-17th century Europe
example: During the Renaissance, artists like Leonardo da Vinci created masterpieces that still amaze people today.

definition: to fill something up again after it has been used
example: We need to replenish our water bottles before starting the second half of the hike.

definition: a place where things are stored or can be found
example: The museum is a repository of ancient artifacts from civilizations around the world.

definition: to hold back or prevent the expression of feelings or actions
example: She tried to repress her laughter during the serious meeting, but a giggle escaped.

definition: to express disappointment in someone for doing something wrong
example: His mother looked at him with reproach when she saw the broken vase on the floor.

definition: to refuse to accept or be associated with something
example: The scientist repudiated the false claims made about her research in the newspaper.

definition: needed for a specific purpose
example: Good hiking boots are a requisite piece of gear for any serious mountain trek.

definition: a large natural or artificial lake used for storing water
example: The reservoir holds enough water to supply the entire city for six months.

definition: remaining after the main part has been removed or used
example: Even after scrubbing, there was residual paint on his hands that wouldn't come off.

definition: able to recover quickly from problems; tough and flexible
example: Bamboo is incredibly resilient—it bends in strong winds but almost never breaks.

definition: the quality of being deep and full, or of evoking strong feelings
example: The story about the rescue dog had an emotional resonance that made everyone in the room tear up.

definition: payment or action to make up for wrongdoing or damage
example: The boy had to pay restitution for the neighbor's window he broke with his baseball.

definition: to pull back or take back something previously said
example: After checking his facts, the reporter had to retract his incorrect story about the mayor.

definition: looking back on past events with the knowledge you have now
example: In retrospect, she realized that studying every night had been the key to her success.

definition: the art of using language well to persuade people
example: The candidate's strong rhetoric convinced many undecided voters to support her.

definition: very thorough, strict, and demanding
example: The rigorous training program required athletes to practice five hours every single day.

definition: a list of names showing who is part of a team or group
example: The coach posted the team roster on the bulletin board, so players could check if they made the cut.

definition: a set of guidelines used for grading work
example: The rubric showed exactly how many points each section of the essay was worth.

definition: to break open or burst suddenly
example: The water pipe will rupture if the heat drops below freezing, and the water inside expands.

definition: clean and safe from germs and disease
example: Restaurant kitchens must meet strict sanitary standards, so customers stay healthy.

definition: a metal frame workers stand on to reach high parts of a building
example: Workers stood on the scaffold to paint the outside of the three-story building.

definition: the enforced separation of different groups of people
example: Rosa Parks fought against segregation by refusing to give up her bus seat.

definition: caused by earthquakes or shaking underground
example: The seismic sensors detected underground movement hours before the volcano erupted.

definition: a small class or meeting for discussion and learning
example: The writing seminar had only twelve students, so everyone could share their work.

definition: a feeling or opinion about something
example: The broad sentiment among students was that the school carnival should happen twice a year.

definition: a person who questions whether something is true or real
example: The skeptic asked to see the proof before believing the magician's claim of real magic.

definition: unity and support among members of a group
example: The whole class showed solidarity with their sick friend by sending cards and flowers.

definition: alone; done without anyone else
example: The solitary lighthouse stood alone on the rocky cliff, guiding ships through the fog.

definition: the authority of a country to govern itself on its own
example: Every nation's sovereignty means it has the right to make its own laws without outside control.

definition: happening by nature and without planning
example: The spontaneous applause surprised the singer—he hadn't expected anyone to clap mid-song.

definition: not moving or flowing; not changing or growing
example: The stagnant pond water turned green with algae because there was no current to keep it fresh.

definition: something that triggers a response
example: The loud noise was a stimulus that made every bird in the tree fly away instantly.

definition: to state exactly what must be done as part of an agreement
example: The rules stipulate that every player must be at least eight years old to enter the contest.

definition: a layer of rock, soil, or material, or a level of society
example: Each stratum of rock in the Grand Canyon tells the story of a different era in Earth's history.

definition: having just barely enough to stay alive
example: Subsistence farmers grow just enough food to feed their own families with nothing left to sell.

definition: to provide evidence that proves something is true
example: She used three different sources to substantiate her claim that dolphins can know themselves.

definition: the right to vote in elections
example: Women in the United States gained suffrage in 1920 when the 19th Amendment was passed.

definition: more than what is needed; unneeded
example: The extra paragraph was superfluous—the essay was already complete without it.

definition: to put an end to something by force, or to hold back feelings
example: She had to suppress a yawn during the long speech, so she wouldn't seem rude.

definition: close watching of someone or something, especially by police or cameras
example: Security cameras provide surveillance of the parking lot twenty-four hours a day.

definition: able to keep on without using up resources or causing damage
example: Using solar power is sustainable because the sun will keep shining for billions of years.

definition: a tax on goods imported from another country
example: The tariff on foreign steel made imported metal more expensive than locally produced steel.

definition: the science of classifying living things into groups
example: In taxonomy, all living things are sorted from broad kingdoms down to individual species.

definition: the period of time someone holds a position
example: During her ten-year tenure as principal, she transformed the school into the best in the district.

definition: evidence of something's quality or truth
example: His perfect score was a testament to months of dedicated studying every single night.

definition: a mathematical statement that has been proven true
example: The Pythagorean theorem shows how to find the longest side of a right triangle.

definition: to formally give up a belief or claim
example: The knight had to abjure his loyalty to the old king before serving the new one.

definition: growth or increase by gradual addition
example: The accretion of snow on the roof made the family worry about its weight.

definition: to make a formal judgment or decision about a dispute
example: The teacher was asked to adjudicate the argument between the two students.

definition: to make something less pure by adding lower quality substances
example: Adding water to the juice would adulterate its natural flavor.

definition: to increase the power, status, or wealth of someone
example: The emperor tried to aggrandize his kingdom by conquering neighboring lands.

definition: the highest point of development or the perfect example
example: The final symphony was the apotheosis of the composer's musical career.

definition: formal approval or praise
example: The student's project received approbation from each teacher in the school.

definition: to take something without having the right to do so
example: The older sibling tried to arrogate control of the television remote each evening.

definition: a return to an earlier, more primitive type
example: The sudden appearance of spots on the horse was considered an atavism.

definition: something that shows what will happen in the future
example: The early election results in that county are a bellwether for the whole state.

definition: to divide into two branches or parts
example: The road will bifurcate at the next intersection, going north and south.

definition: to remove parts of a book or text that are considered offensive
example: The publisher chose to bowdlerize the old story to make it suitable for children.

definition: a remark that is meant to comfort but sounds boring and unoriginal
example: Telling someone 'time heals all wounds' is a common bromide that rarely helps.

definition: the rise and fall of the voice when speaking or a rhythmic pattern
example: The poet read with a beautiful cadence that made the words sound like music.

definition: a small secret group that plans to do something harmful
example: The story was about a cabal of wizards who plotted to steal the king's crown.

definition: a false story or rumor that is spread to trick people
example: The canard about the school closing turned out to be completely untrue.

definition: surrendering or giving in when you cannot win
example: The team's capitulation to the opposing side ended the long debate.

definition: speed and quickness of movement
example: The fire crew responded with celerity and arrived at the scene in minutes.

definition: to think hard and carefully, using deep focus
example: She sat quietly to cogitate on the best solution to the difficult problem.

definition: to secretly work together to act dishonestly
example: The two students colluded to copy each other's answers during the test.

definition: to combine two or more things into one, often incorrectly
example: It is easy to conflate the words 'affect' and 'effect' because they sound alike.

definition: the state of being related by blood
example: The family tree showed the consanguinity between the cousins.

definition: sharing a border or touching along an edge
example: The two contiguous states shared a long border along the river.

definition: to cancel or reverse an order or command
example: The general chose to countermand the previous order and retreat instead.

definition: causing harm, especially slowly over time
example: Too much screen time can have deleterious effects on a child's sleep.

definition: a long and angry speech or piece of writing criticizing something
example: The letter to the editor was a diatribe against the new parking rules.

definition: works well; gets the job done
example: The new cleaning product was surprisingly efficacious at removing tough stains.

definition: to criticize someone very harshly
example: The food critic excoriated the restaurant for its poor service and cold food.

definition: needing action right now; urgent
example: The exigent situation demanded that everyone evacuate the building immediately.

definition: to remove objectionable content from a text
example: The school chose to expurgate certain words from the play before performing it.

definition: to fully destroy or remove something
example: The gardener worked hard to extirpate the weeds from the flower bed.

definition: silly and pointless
example: His fatuous suggestion to fly to school was met with eye rolls from everyone.

definition: producing a lot of new ideas, plants, or offspring
example: The fecund garden overflowed with tomatoes, peppers, and squash all summer.

definition: to express strong protest or criticism loudly
example: The coach began to fulminate about the unfair referee calls during the game.

definition: to deny or contradict something
example: No one could gainsay the evidence that the test had succeeded.

definition: the practice of drawing voting district maps in a tricky way to help one side win
example: Gerrymandering can unfairly influence election results by changing district lines.

definition: having very little money; poor
example: The impecunious artist could barely afford paint and canvas for her work.

definition: impossible to avoid or escape
example: The ineluctable conclusion was that the old bridge needed to be replaced.

definition: harmful or hostile to something
example: The dry weather was inimical to the growth of the young trees.

definition: impossible to understand or interpret
example: The cat's inscrutable expression gave no clue about what it was thinking.

definition: destructive to both sides in a conflict
example: The internecine rivalry between the two departments hurt the whole firm.

definition: a person who does something wrong or illegal, especially in public office
example: The malfeasant official was removed from his position after the investigation.

definition: stubbornly refusing to change one's view or behavior
example: The obdurate negotiator would not agree to any compromise whatsoever.

definition: harsh criticism or public disgrace
example: The cheating scandal brought opprobrium upon the entire team.

definition: something reused or altered but still showing traces of its earlier form
example: The old wall was a palimpsest of layers of paint from different decades.

definition: expressing a strong view, especially a controversial one
example: The tendentious article clearly favored one political party over the other.

definition: a harsh, bad-tempered person
example: The termagant shopkeeper shouted at each buyer who touched the merchandise.

definition: expressed strongly and clearly; cutting
example: Her trenchant analysis of the problem helped the team find a fix quickly.

definition: having no purpose or value
example: The committee's nugatory efforts produced no results after months of meetings.

definition: a speech or text that praises someone or something highly
example: The retirement speech was a heartfelt panegyric about the teacher's thirty-year career.

definition: to manage or be in charge of something
example: The vice principal will administer the school while the principal is away.

definition: a person who is on the other side or fights you
example: In chess, your adversary sits across the board planning their next move.

definition: having a lot of money; wealthy
example: The affluent neighborhood had large houses with swimming pools.

definition: to catch or arrest someone
example: The police were able to apprehend the thief before he left the mall.

definition: a strong hope or goal you work toward
example: His aspiration to become an astronaut drove him to study science every day.

definition: to make something greater by adding to it
example: Adding another shelf will augment the storage space in the closet.

definition: plain and not comfortable; without extra decoration
example: The monk's austere room had only a bed, a table, and a single candle.

definition: to prove something is real
example: The museum expert could authenticate the painting as real.

definition: to turn away or prevent something bad
example: She averted an argument by changing the subject.

definition: kind and generous; wanting to help others
example: The benevolent donor gave a million dollars to build a children's hospital.

definition: the range of plant and animal life in an area
example: The rainforest has incredible biodiversity with thousands of different species.

definition: truthful and direct; saying what you really think
example: Her candid review of the movie said it was boring despite the great special effects.

definition: a system where people own their own businesses
example: Under capitalism, people can start their own companies and earn profits.

definition: the distance around the outside of a circle
example: We measured the circumference of the basketball using a piece of string.

definition: a loud noise or strong demand from many people
example: There was a clamor in the cafeteria when someone spotted a bird inside.

definition: to force someone to do something against their will
example: The bully tried to coerce younger kids into giving up their lunch money.

definition: to be made up of; to include
example: The United States comprises many states and several territories.

definition: happening at the same time
example: The two concerts were concurrent, so we had to choose which one to attend.

definition: to discuss something with others before deciding
example: The judges needed to confer before announcing the winner.

definition: to take something away as punishment or by authority
example: The teacher had to confiscate the toy because it was distracting the class.

definition: careful and thorough in doing work
example: The conscientious student double-checked each answer before turning in the test.

definition: the legal right to control how creative work is used
example: The author holds the copyright to her novel, so no one can copy it.

definition: to have a connection between two things
example: Scientists found that more exercise tends to correlate with better sleep.

definition: to reach the highest point or final result
example: Months of rehearsal will culminate in the big performance on Friday night.

definition: to reduce or limit something
example: Bad weather forced the school to curtail outdoor recess to just ten minutes.

definition: to consider or judge something in a certain way
example: The principal deemed the hallway mural appropriate and allowed it to stay.

definition: to invent or plan something clever
example: The kids had to devise a plan to rescue their kite from the tall tree.

definition: to force something or someone out of its usual place
example: The flood will displace hundreds of families from their homes.

definition: designed to be thrown away after one use
example: The cafeteria switched from disposable plates to reusable trays.

definition: a range of different types of people or things
example: Our school celebrates the diversity of cultures represented by its students.

definition: to tame a wild animal for human use
example: Thousands of years ago humans began to domesticate wolves into dogs.

definition: having the most power or control
example: The lion's dominance over the pride means it eats first.

definition: an official ban on trading with a country
example: The embargo prevented any ships from delivering goods to that nation.

definition: a person who starts and runs their own business
example: The young entrepreneur sold handmade bracelets at the farmers market.

definition: to list things one by one
example: The teacher asked us to enumerate all the planets in our solar system.

definition: fair and equal for all involved
example: The teacher found an equitable way to divide the art supplies among all groups.

definition: a moral principle that guides a person's behavior
example: His strong work ethic meant he always finished his homework before playing.

definition: to bring a feeling or memory to mind
example: The smell of cookies can evoke happy memories of visiting grandma's house.

definition: a short passage taken from a longer work
example: The teacher read an excerpt from the novel to get students interested.

definition: to make something up or invent a false story
example: He tried to fabricate an excuse for being late, but the teacher didn't believe him.

definition: about budgets, taxes, and how money is managed
example: The school's fiscal year begins in September when the new budget starts.

definition: most important or leading
example: She is the foremost expert on dinosaurs at the natural history museum.

definition: to create or develop a plan or idea with care
example: The detective needed time to formulate a theory about who committed the crime.

definition: a crack or break, especially in a bone
example: The X-ray showed a small fracture in her wrist from the skateboard fall.

definition: a business given permission to sell another company's products
example: The ice cream shop is a franchise with identical stores in fifty cities.

definition: to measure or guess something
example: It's hard to gauge how long the trip will take without checking the map.

definition: the way a group or country is managed
example: Good governance means leaders make fair decisions that help all.

definition: to move to a new country to live there permanently
example: Her grandparents chose to immigrate to another country for better chances.

definition: about to happen very soon
example: The dark clouds meant a thunderstorm was imminent.

definition: very important and urgent
example: It is imperative that everyone evacuate the building during a fire drill.

definition: to show that someone is involved in something wrong
example: The fingerprints on the cookie jar implicate the youngest brother.

definition: suggested but not directly stated
example: There was an implicit understanding that everyone would help clean up after the party.

definition: the process of asking questions to find answers or info
example: The detective's inquiry into the missing bike led him to check every garage.

definition: to become stronger or more extreme
example: The storm will intensify overnight with winds reaching 80 miles per hour.

definition: the advantage or power that helps you influence a situation
example: Having more facts gave the team leverage in the debate.

definition: the engine that pulls a train
example: The strong locomotive pulled fifty freight cars along the mountain tracks.

definition: to attract or tempt someone toward something
example: The bright colors of the candy display lure children into the store.

definition: to make as large or great as possible
example: To maximize your score, answer every question even if you're not sure.

definition: a way of sharing information or ideas with others
example: The internet has become the main medium for sharing news.

definition: a thin layer that covers or separates things
example: The cell membrane controls what goes in and out of each cell.

definition: to reduce to the smallest possible amount
example: Wearing sunscreen helps minimize the risk of sunburn.

definition: so small that it's not worth worrying about
example: The difference between their test scores was negligible—only one point.

definition: a group of connected people, computers, or things
example: The city has a network of underground tunnels connecting all the buildings.

definition: very small; in name only
example: The museum charges a nominal fee of one dollar to help cover costs.

definition: a reason for disagreeing with something
example: Her main objection to the plan was that it would take too long.

definition: to require or force someone to do something
example: The contract will obligate you to mow the lawn every Saturday.

definition: no longer useful because something better exists
example: Typewriters became obsolete once computers became widely available.

definition: the best or most favorable
example: The optimum temperature for growing tomatoes is around 75 degrees.

definition: to watch over and direct work being done
example: The construction manager will oversee the building of the new gym.

definition: a rule or limit that sets what is allowed
example: One parameter of the test was that all plants must get equal sunlight.

definition: joining in and being part of an activity
example: Class participation counts for twenty percent of your final grade.

definition: a collection of someone's work, shown to others
example: The artist brought her portfolio of paintings to the gallery interview.

definition: coming before something else
example: Prior to starting fifth grade, she read all summer to prepare.

definition: an estimate of what will happen in the future
example: The weather projection shows sunny skies for the rest of the week.

definition: to spread or reproduce
example: Dandelions propagate by sending fluffy seeds floating through the wind.

definition: a period when the economy shrinks and people have less money
example: During the recession many stores closed because people stopped shopping.

definition: to improve something by making small changes
example: He continued to refine his essay until every sentence was clear and strong.

definition: dependence on something
example: Our reliance on smartphones means we feel lost without them.

definition: the feeling of not wanting to do something
example: His reluctance to try new foods meant he always ordered the same thing.

definition: careful and detailed examination
example: The judge examined each contestant's artwork with close scrutiny before choosing a winner.

definition: a brother or sister
example: She is the oldest of four siblings, with two brothers and one sister.

definition: to state exactly and clearly
example: Please specify which flavor of ice cream you want—vanilla or chocolate.

definition: a position or attitude on an issue
example: Her stance on homework was that it should never take more than thirty minutes.

definition: the state of being responsible for your actions
example: Students learn accountability by turning in their own homework on time.

definition: caring about others more than yourself
example: Her altruistic nature led her to donate all her birthday money to charity.

definition: a warning or condition to keep in mind
example: The free trial came with one caveat: you must cancel before thirty days.

definition: to accept behavior that most people think is wrong
example: The school does not condone bullying of any kind.

definition: a lack or shortage of something
example: The dearth of rain caused the flowers to wilt in the garden.

definition: final, complete, and most trusted
example: The encyclopedia was the definitive guide to world animals.

definition: slowly decreasing or fading
example: Interest in the old playground was ebbing as the new one opened.

definition: to represent an idea in a visible form
example: The statue embodies the spirit of freedom and courage.

definition: to publicly support a belief or thought
example: The mayor espoused the importance of keeping the river clean.

definition: able to be predicted or expected
example: There are no snow days in the foreseeable future.

definition: to make it difficult for something to happen
example: Loud noise from the street hindered our ability to concentrate.

definition: not leading to a firm decision or result
example: The test was inconclusive, so we had to try again.

definition: absolutely necessary; cannot do without
example: Clean water is indispensable for survival.

definition: introducing new and creative thoughts or methods
example: The student's innovative design made the robot move faster.

definition: done in an organized and careful way
example: His methodical approach to cleaning meant each corner was spotless.

definition: having many different aspects or sides
example: Climate change is a multifaceted problem affecting food, water, and weather.

definition: to cancel out or make something have no effect
example: Eating a whole cake would negate the benefits of exercising.

definition: required by a rule or law
example: Wearing a helmet is obligatory when riding a motorcycle.

definition: the best possible for a given situation
example: The optimal temperature for baking bread is 375 degrees.

definition: the state of being equal
example: The league worked for parity so every team had a fair chance to win.

definition: something needed before you can do something else
example: Passing the swim test is a prerequisite for joining the diving team.

definition: so high in cost that it prevents action
example: The prohibitive price of the toy meant she had to save for months.

definition: matching in size or amount relative to something else
example: The amount of paint should be proportional to the size of the wall.

definition: done in accordance with a rule or agreement
example: Pursuant to school policy, all visitors must sign in at the office.

definition: to prove that something is wrong
example: She refuted the claim by showing her receipts as evidence.

definition: belonging separately to each one mentioned
example: The students returned to their respective classrooms after lunch.

definition: to officially cancel or take away a right
example: The school may revoke your library card if you lose too many books.

definition: to ask for something from someone
example: Volunteers went door to door to solicit donations for the food bank.

definition: required or established by law
example: The statutory speed limit on this road is thirty miles per hour.

definition: to be enough for a particular purpose
example: A simple apology will suffice—you don't need to buy a gift.

definition: a sudden powerful increase
example: A surge of wind knocked the kite out of his hands.

definition: to guess based on limited proof
example: From the empty plate, she surmised that someone had eaten lunch.

definition: able to be touched or felt; real and concrete
example: The trophy was tangible proof of the team's hard work.

definition: very weak or slight; not strong
example: The connection between the two clues was tenuous at best.

definition: to support or maintain a law or belief
example: The court chose to uphold the original ruling.

definition: to hold and use a tool or power
example: The blacksmith wielded a heavy hammer to shape the red-hot metal.

definition: very bad or of very poor quality
example: The team's abysmal record of zero wins disappointed all.

definition: to increase over time through gradual addition
example: Interest will accrue in your savings account each month.

definition: angry and bitter in tone
example: The acrimonious debate left both sides feeling upset.

definition: clever and skillful
example: The adroit magician made the coin vanish from his hand.

definition: happening or being planned
example: Something mysterious was afoot when all the lights went out.

definition: to worry greatly about a choice
example: He agonized over which dress to wear to the school dance.

definition: to reduce fear or worry
example: The teacher's calm words helped allay the students' fears about the test.

definition: distant and not friendly; keeping to yourself
example: The new student seemed aloof, sitting alone at lunch every day.

definition: a mixture or blend of different things
example: The city's culture is an amalgam of traditions from many countries.

definition: a useful or pleasant feature of a place
example: The hotel's amenities included a pool, gym, and free breakfast.

definition: having no clear shape or structure
example: The amorphous blob of clay waited to be shaped into a vase.

definition: severe pain or suffering, especially emotional
example: The anguish of losing her pet was written all over her face.

definition: calm confidence, even in a stressful moment
example: She handled the surprise quiz with aplomb, finishing first.

definition: boring because it has been said too many times
example: The speech was full of banal phrases like 'try your best.'

definition: to give a false impression of something
example: Her calm smile belied the nervousness she felt inside.

definition: to scold someone angrily and at length
example: The coach berated the team for not trying hard enough.

definition: missing something important; without what you need
example: The garden looked bereft of color after the frost destroyed all the flowers.

definition: very clear and done without shame
example: Copying answers from a neighbor is a blatant form of cheating.

definition: showing a carefree lack of concern
example: His blithe attitude about the test worried his parents.

definition: using impressive words that sound important but mean little
example: The politician's bombastic speech was full of big words but few ideas.

definition: to wave something around to show it off or as a threat
example: He brandished his umbrella to scare off the goose.

definition: bold and shameless
example: The brazen squirrel stole a sandwich right off the picnic table.

definition: showing no concern for others' feelings
example: His callous remark about her painting made her cry.

definition: to express strong disapproval officially
example: The student council voted to censure the member who broke the rules.

definition: very poor; unwilling to spend money
example: The penurious family could barely afford groceries each week.

definition: influence or power, especially in politics
example: The principal had enough clout to change the school lunch menu.

definition: a strongbox for money; funds or treasury
example: The club's coffers were empty after paying for the field trip.

definition: to behave in a particular way
example: She comported herself with dignity during the award event.

definition: to deliver or hand over something permanently
example: Old toys were consigned to the donation box.

definition: to interpret the meaning of something
example: His silence was construed as agreement by the rest of the group.

definition: tending to destroy or wear away slowly
example: The corrosive acid slowly ate through the metal container.

definition: to publicly criticize something as bad or wrong
example: Environmental groups decried the plan to cut down the old forest.

definition: to make someone believe something that is not true
example: Don't delude yourself into thinking you can pass without studying.

definition: to feel strong disapproval of something
example: Teachers deplore cheating because it hurts everyone's learning.

definition: a ruler who uses power in a cruel way
example: The despot forced the people to work without pay.

definition: intended to teach a moral lesson
example: The didactic fable taught children the value of honesty.

definition: shy and lacking self-confidence
example: The diffident boy spoke so quietly that nobody could hear him.

definition: very different from each other
example: The two sisters had disparate interests—one loved sports, the other art.

definition: to teach someone and make them wiser or better
example: The documentary was designed to edify viewers about ocean life.

definition: abnormally thin and weak from lack of food
example: The emaciated stray cat was taken to a shelter for care.

definition: to involve someone in a conflict or difficult situation
example: The argument embroiled the entire class in a heated debate.

definition: to weigh down or make movement difficult
example: The heavy backpack encumbered the hiker on the steep trail.

definition: unclear or having more than one meaning
example: His equivocal answer left everyone unsure whether he agreed or not.

definition: to cause someone to become unfriendly or distant
example: The argument estranged the two best friends for an entire year.

definition: to strongly urge someone to do something
example: The principal exhorted students to read at least one book a week.

definition: to make a fault seem less serious
example: Her apology helped extenuate the offense in the teacher's eyes.

definition: to praise something with excitement
example: The teacher extolled the benefits of reading every single day.

definition: treating serious issues with deliberately inappropriate humor
example: His facetious comment about the test made everyone laugh nervously.

definition: well chosen; perfectly suited
example: The teacher made a felicitous comparison between rivers and time.

definition: to restrict or restrain someone's freedom
example: Too many rules can fetter a student's new ideas.

definition: changing opinions or loyalty a lot
example: The fickle weather changed from sunny to rainy three times today.

definition: to stir up or encourage trouble
example: The troublemaker tried to foment a food fight in the cafeteria.

definition: secretive and trying to avoid being noticed
example: The furtive glance at his neighbor's test paper was caught by the teacher.

definition: lacking social grace; awkward
example: It was gauche to start eating before everyone was seated at the table.

definition: friendly and cheerful
example: The genial host greeted every guest with a warm handshake.

definition: to collect information bit by bit
example: She gleaned useful facts from several different library books.

definition: an outward appearance used to hide the truth
example: The spy entered the party in the guise of a waiter.

definition: having no luck; always running into bad situations
example: The hapless traveler lost his wallet, then missed his flight.

definition: very frightening or distressing
example: The harrowing rescue of the trapped miners was shown on the news.

definition: behaving in a proud and unfriendly way
example: The haughty queen refused to speak with the common villagers.

definition: very peaceful and pleasant
example: The idyllic village had flower gardens and a quiet stream.

definition: without any faults; perfect
example: His impeccable spelling earned his first place in the spelling bee.

definition: impossible to satisfy or calm down
example: The implacable critic never had a kind word for any performance.

definition: impossible to defeat or capture
example: The castle's thick walls made it seem impregnable to attackers.

definition: behavior that is not proper or acceptable
example: Talking loudly during the ceremony was considered an impropriety.

definition: not showing signs of future success
example: The inauspicious start to the game—two errors in the first inning—worried the fans.

definition: impossible to correct or improve
example: The incorrigible puppy kept chewing shoes no matter what we tried.

definition: unwilling or unable to believe something
example: She was incredulous when told she had won the grand prize.

definition: never getting tired or giving up
example: The indefatigable runner completed five marathons in one year.

definition: impossible to remove or forget
example: The trip to the Grand Canyon left an indelible impression on her mind.

definition: wanting to avoid action or work; lazy
example: The indolent cat spent the entire day sleeping on the couch.

definition: lacking skill; clumsy
example: His inept attempt at juggling sent all three balls rolling away.

definition: very difficult to deal with or solve
example: The intractable math problem stumped even the brightest students.

definition: annoying and irritating
example: The irksome buzzing of the fly made it hard to concentrate.

definition: having or showing good judgment
example: A judicious use of her allowance let her save enough for the bike.

definition: existing but not yet visible or active
example: His latent talent for singing was discovered during music class.

definition: to praise highly
example: Critics lauded the young author for her brilliant first novel.

definition: lacking energy; sluggish
example: The hot day made the students feel lethargic during afternoon classes.

definition: humor or lack of seriousness, especially when it is not expected
example: A bit of levity from the teacher helped ease the tension before the exam.

definition: very high and impressive
example: The lofty mountain peaks were covered in snow year-round.

definition: so foolish or unfair that it is amusing
example: The idea of keeping a pet elephant in a tiny apartment is ludicrous.

definition: to speak about someone in a harmful and untrue way
example: It is wrong to malign someone behind their back.

definition: easily shaped or influenced
example: Gold is one of the most malleable metals on Earth.

definition: deserving reward or praise
example: The student received an award for meritorious service to the town.

definition: a wrong or inaccurate name
example: Calling a dolphin a fish is a misnomer because it is a mammal.

definition: a small amount of something
example: With a modicum of effort, you can keep your room tidy.

definition: lacking excitement; ordinary
example: Washing dishes is a mundane chore that still needs to be done.

definition: so surprised that you do not know how to react
example: She was nonplussed when the principal announced a surprise holiday.

definition: harmful or poisonous
example: Noxious fumes from the factory polluted the nearby lake.

definition: a subtle difference in meaning or quality
example: Understanding the nuance between 'happy' and 'elated' improves your writing.

definition: slow to understand; missing obvious hints
example: He was being obtuse on purpose, pretending not to understand the joke.

definition: very unpleasant; hateful
example: The odious smell from the garbage made everyone cover their noses.

definition: having unlimited power
example: In the story, the wizard was omnipotent and could control the weather.

definition: happening at a very good or convenient time
example: The rain stopped at an opportune moment, just before the outdoor concert.

definition: resembling a palace; grand and spacious
example: The palatial mansion had thirty rooms, and a marble staircase.

definition: to make something less severe without removing the cause
example: Medicine can palliate the pain even if it cannot cure the illness.

definition: so strong that it can almost be felt or touched
example: The tension in the room was palpable as the winner was announced.

definition: giving a wide unobstructed view
example: The panoramic view from the mountaintop showed the entire valley.

definition: an outcast; someone avoided by others
example: After telling on his teammates, he became a pariah in the group.

definition: showing a calm, peaceful countryside scene
example: The pastoral landscape of green hills and grazing sheep calmed her mind.

definition: a quality that evokes pity or sadness
example: The ending of the movie was full of pathos that moved the audience to tears.

definition: childishly bad-tempered and sulky
example: The petulant child stomped his feet when he did not get dessert.

definition: the string on a bow used to shoot arrows
example: He pulled back the bowstring and let the arrow fly.

definition: a tall plant with bell-shaped flowers
example: The foxglove grew next to the garden wall.

definition: a small pot that holds ink for writing
example: He dipped his pen into the inkwell.

definition: a U-shaped bend in a river
example: The river formed an oxbow around the hill.

definition: to shed skin, feathers, or fur
example: The snake molted its old skin and grew a new one.

definition: not living; non-living parts of nature
example: Rocks and water are abiotic parts of the environment.

definition: to leave secretly and quickly
example: The thief absconded with the money from the cash box.

definition: a small, thick patch of trees or bushes
example: Birds nested in the copse near the river.

definition: to dry out completely
example: The hot sun desiccated the fruit until it was like a chip.

definition: to erase or wipe out
example: Rain effaced the chalk drawing on the sidewalk.

definition: wanting to fight or argue
example: His bellicose comments turned a small disagreement into an argument.

definition: carefully planned to achieve a specific goal
example: Placing her strongest player at center was a strategic choice that won the game.

definition: to divide something into smaller parts
example: The developer plans to subdivide the large farm into twenty home lots.

definition: one right after the next without gap
example: It rained for five successive days canceling all outdoor activities.

definition: not certain or fixed; done without confidence
example: We made tentative plans for Saturday, but they might change depending on the weather.

definition: living on land rather than in water or air
example: Frogs begin life in water but become terrestrial animals as adults.

definition: the main argument or thought in a piece of writing
example: The thesis of her essay was that schools should start later in the morning.

definition: the level at which something begins to happen
example: Once the temperature crosses the freezing threshold, water turns to ice.

definition: the curved path of something moving through air
example: The ball's trajectory curved left and landed in the neighbor's yard.

definition: to go beyond the limits of something
example: Great music can transcend language barriers and move people everywhere.

definition: of very little importance
example: Don't worry about that trivial spelling error—the thoughts in your essay are great.

definition: a machine with blades turned by wind, water, or steam
example: The wind turbine's giant blades spin to generate clean power.

definition: to bring together into one
example: The coach worked to unify the team after the players had an argument.

definition: the area near or surrounding a place
example: There are three pizza shops in the vicinity of our school.

definition: by which; through which method
example: They created a system whereby students earn points for good behavior.

definition: rough enough to wear things away; also harsh in manner
example: The abrasive sandpaper smoothed the wooden table until it felt silky.

definition: to choose not to do or have something
example: Three members of the committee decided to abstain from the vote.

definition: easy to reach, enter, or use
example: The new ramp makes the building accessible to people in wheelchairs.

definition: enthusiastic approval and praise
example: The young author received acclaim for his first novel from critics everywhere.

definition: in charge and expected to explain your actions
example: Team captains are accountable for making sure everyone follows the rules.

definition: getting or gaining something new
example: The museum's latest acquisition is a rare painting from the 1700s.

definition: refusing to change your mind about something
example: She was adamant that pineapple belongs on pizza no matter what others said.

definition: to firmly warn or scold someone
example: The librarian had to admonish the students for being too loud.

definition: harmful or unfavorable
example: Adverse weather conditions forced the pilot to delay the flight.

definition: about beauty, design, and good taste
example: The aesthetic design of the new park includes fountains and flower gardens.

definition: an official connection to a group
example: Her affiliation with the robotics club gave her access to the lab after school.

definition: to make a problem worse or to annoy someone
example: Scratching a mosquito bite will only aggravate the itching.

definition: able to move quickly and with ease
example: The agile guard changed direction so fast that the defender fell over.

definition: although; even though
example: The cake was delicious, albeit a little too sweet for my taste.

definition: a claim that someone has done something wrong
example: The allegation that someone cheated on the test was investigated by the principal.

definition: to make pain or a problem less severe
example: Drinking hot tea can alleviate a sore throat and help you feel better.

definition: to combine or unite into one
example: The two small schools chose to amalgamate into one larger school.

definition: a comparison showing how two things are alike
example: She used the analogy of a factory to explain how the human body works.

definition: to add land to an existing country or area; also an extra building
example: The library annex next door has a quiet study room for older students.

definition: something that is different from what is normal
example: Finding a tropical fish in the cold lake was a strange anomaly.

definition: a collection of stories or poems in one book
example: The anthology contained short stories from twenty different authors.

definition: to calm someone down by giving them what they want
example: Mom gave the toddler a cracker to appease him while dinner was cooking.

definition: extra facts at the end of a book; also a body organ
example: The appendix at the back of the textbook contains all the formulas.

definition: to assess the value or quality of something
example: The expert came to appraise the old coins to see how much they're worth.

definition: anxious or fearful about something in the future
example: He felt apprehensive about his first day at the new school.

definition: right or suitable; also having a natural tendency
example: His comparison of the crowded hallway to a beehive was very apt.

definition: suitable for growing crops
example: The valley has rich, arable land that farmers use to grow wheat and corn.

definition: requiring a lot of effort; hard and tiring
example: The arduous hike to the summit took eight hours of steep climbing.

definition: to go up or climb
example: The hikers began to ascend the rocky trail at sunrise.

definition: to absorb and understand new facts or culture
example: It takes time to assimilate all the new vocabulary you learn each week.

definition: a very cruel or awful act
example: The documentary described the atrocity of slavery in American history.

definition: a quality or feature of something
example: Patience is an important attribute for anyone who wants to teach children.

definition: an official examination of accounts or records
example: The school conducted an audit to make sure all fundraising money was properly spent.

definition: trusted and respected as accurate
example: The encyclopedia is considered an authoritative source for research papers.

definition: a strong dislike or desire to avoid something

definition: the capacity for data transfer; also available time or power
example: The school's internet bandwidth was upgraded so videos load faster.

definition: a tool that measures air pressure; also an indicator

definition: to divide into two equal parts
example: Draw a line to bisect the circle into two perfect halves.

definition: a group of countries or people with shared interests
example: The trading bloc agreed to remove taxes on goods between member countries.

definition: shortness of time or expression
example: The teacher praised the brevity of his answer—clear and to the point.

definition: the very edge of something, especially before a big change
example: The endangered species was on the brink of extinction until conservationists helped.

definition: a state official who follows rigid rules
example: The bureaucrat insisted every form be filled out in triplicate.

definition: most important; also a bright red songbird
example: The cardinal rule of the science lab is to always wear safety goggles.

definition: soldiers who fight on horseback
example: The cavalry charged across the battlefield on their strong horses.

definition: to find a way around a problem or rule

definition: something pledged as security for a loan; also additional or secondary

definition: shared by all members of a community
example: The communal garden is tended by all in the apartment building.

definition: alike enough to be compared
example: The two phones are comparable in quality but differ in price.

definition: having enough skill to do something well
example: After months of training, she became a competent swimmer who could do all four strokes.

definition: too satisfied with things as they are, not trying to improve
example: The team became complacent after winning and lost their next three games.

definition: expressing much in few words; brief and clear
example: A concise summary should capture the main points in just a few sentences.

definition: making something likely or possible
example: A quiet room with good lighting is conducive to focused studying.

definition: a word that connects parts of a sentence; also a combination
example: The words 'and,' 'but,' and 'or' are common conjunctions in English.

definition: taking over a place or people by force
example: The Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire changed the Americas for all time.

definition: a voter in a certain district; also a component of something
example: The senator listened to concerns from her constituents at the town hall meeting.

definition: goods that are illegal to import or possess
example: Airport security checks bags for contraband like weapons or banned items.

definition: to come together from different directions at one point

definition: to slowly destroy metal through chemical reaction

definition: a sudden seizure of power from a government
example: The military attempted a coup to overthrow the elected leader.

definition: secret or hidden
example: The covert operation was planned in secret so no one would find out.

definition: making decisions quickly and firmly; settling an issue
example: Her decisive three-pointer in the last second won the whole tournament.

definition: a formal or official statement
example: The Declaration of Independence stated that all people have equal rights.

definition: an official order issued by someone in power
example: The king issued a decree that all children must attend school.

definition: to figure something out using logic and proof
example: From the wet footprints, he could deduce that someone had just entered.

definition: what happens on its own if you don't make a change
example: The phone's default ringtone plays unless you choose a different one.

definition: to postpone or yield to someone else's judgment
example: The students chose to defer the vote until everyone was present.

definition: the process of something getting worse or being damaged
example: Soil degradation from overfarming makes it harder to grow healthy crops.

definition: long and careful consideration before deciding
example: After hours of deliberation, the jury at last reached their verdict.

definition: a young person who regularly breaks rules or laws
example: The program helps delinquent teens get back on track through mentoring.

definition: to decrease in value over time
example: Most cars depreciate quickly and lose half their value in five years.

definition: causing harm or damage
example: Too much screen time can be detrimental to your sleep quality.

definition: to spread out over a wide area
example: Open the windows to diffuse the smell of fresh cookies throughout the house.

definition: to see or recognize something that is not clear
example: It was hard to discern the tiny lizard hiding among the brown leaves.

definition: to treat someone unfairly based on their group identity
example: Laws exist to prevent companies from discriminating against people based on race.

definition: sending someone away or saying no to an idea
example: The dismissal of school was delayed by thirty minutes due to the storm.

definition: to send someone or something quickly to a destination
example: The fire station will dispatch a truck within two minutes of receiving a call.

definition: to spread information widely
example: Social media makes it easy to disseminate news to millions of people instantly.

definition: to separate and go in different directions
example: The hiking trail will diverge at the creek—left goes uphill, right follows the water.

definition: a person who lives in a particular place
example: City dwellers often rely on public transportation instead of cars.

definition: an official order or proclamation
example: The mayor issued an edict banning fireworks within the city limits.

definition: being able to produce the desired result
example: Scientists tested the efficacy of the new drug on a thousand patients.

definition: all the people in a country who are eligible to vote
example: The electorate will choose a new leader in November.

definition: a select group that is the best at something
example: Only the elite athletes who pass qualifying rounds go to the Olympics.

definition: to begin a journey or new project
example: Tomorrow we embark on a two-week road trip across the country.

definition: to try to equal or surpass by imitating
example: Young basketball players often try to emulate their favorite professional athletes.

definition: to make into law or act out
example: Congress voted to enact a law protecting endangered sea turtles.

definition: a serious attempt to do something
example: Learning to play piano is a worthwhile endeavor that takes years of practice.

definition: to provide with a quality or asset
example: The generous family chose to endow the school with a million-dollar prize.

definition: to cause a feeling or situation to exist
example: Working together on the project helped engender trust between the two rivals.

definition: something mysterious and hard to understand
example: How the ancient Egyptians built the pyramids remains an enigma to this day.

definition: to happen afterward as a result
example: When the fire alarm rang, chaos began to ensue as all rushed to the exits.

definition: to involve or require as a needed part
example: The job would entail working weekends and going to different cities.

definition: a period of time marked by notable events
example: The invention of the internet marked a new epoch in human communication.

definition: fairness in treatment; also the value of ownership
example: The school promotes equity by giving extra help to students who need it most.

definition: unpredictable and inconsistent
example: The butterfly's erratic flight path made it impossible to follow.

definition: the most important quality of something
example: The essence of good sportsmanship is respecting your opponent win or lose.

definition: to make a problem or situation worse
example: Yelling at each other will only exacerbate the argument.

definition: to dig up or uncover something from the ground
example: Archaeologists excavate old ruins carefully with tiny brushes.

definition: limited to a certain group; not shared
example: The exclusive club only allows members who pass a special test.

definition: to be a typical example of something
example: Her perfect attendance and high grades exemplify what a dedicated student looks like.

definition: to make something happen more quickly
example: Paying extra for express shipping will expedite delivery to just two days.

definition: increasing more and more rapidly
example: The exponential growth of the bacteria doubled their number each hour.

definition: spending much more than needed; excessive
example: The extravagant birthday party included a live band, and a chocolate fountain.

definition: the front of a building; also a false appearance
example: Behind his tough facade he was actually very shy and sensitive.

definition: to understand something complex; also a unit of water depth
example: I cannot fathom how she memorized the entire periodic table in one week.

definition: an impressive achievement needing great skill or strength
example: Climbing Mount Everest is considered one of the greatest physical feats.

definition: being true and loyal; also how closely a copy matches the original
example: The high-fidelity speakers reproduce music just as it was recorded.

definition: the plants of a specific region or period
example: The guide book describes the flora of the desert, including rare cactus species.

definition: to take possession of land when payments aren't made
example: The bank may foreclose on a house if the owner stops paying the mortgage.

definition: about to happen; also willing to share facts
example: The forthcoming election will determine who leads the city for the next four years.

definition: a place or meeting for public discussion
example: The town forum gave residents a chance to voice their opinions about the new park.

definition: to encourage the development of something
example: Reading to children every night helps foster a love of books and stories.

definition: a person who has escaped from custody or is hiding from the law
example: The fugitive hid in the mountains for weeks before police found him.

definition: pointless; having no chance of success
example: Trying to empty the ocean with a bucket would be a futile effort.

definition: the origin or start of something
example: The genesis of her interest in space was watching a rocket launch as a child.

definition: about how geography affects politics between countries
example: Access to oil creates geopolitical tension between countries in the Middle East.

definition: the main point or general meaning of something
example: I missed the meeting but got the gist of it from reading the notes.

definition: impressive but at times unrealistically ambitious
example: His grandiose plan to build a treehouse city was creative but impractical.

definition: organized by normal people rather than leaders
example: The grassroots campaign collected thousands of signatures from normal citizens.

definition: the feeling of being thankful
example: He expressed his gratitude by writing thank-you cards to every helper.

definition: the total value of goods and services produced by a country
example: A country's gross domestic product shows how strong its economy is.

definition: water found underground in soil and rock
example: Wells pump groundwater up from deep below the surface for drinking.

definition: a fighter who uses surprise attacks rather than open battle
example: Guerrilla fighters used the jungle as cover for their surprise raids.

definition: done regularly as a habit
example: His habitual lateness meant he missed the opening of almost every class.

definition: to slow down or prevent progress
example: Heavy backpacks can hamper students' ability to walk quickly between classes.

definition: a sign of something that is coming
example: The first robin of spring is a harbinger of warmer days ahead.

definition: to control and use a natural force
example: Wind farms harness the power of the wind to create clean electricity.

definition: a safe or peaceful place
example: The quiet library was a haven for students who needed to escape the noise.

definition: dangerous; likely to cause harm
example: The hazardous chemicals must be stored in a locked cabinet in the lab.

definition: passed from parent to child through genes
example: Eye color is hereditary—you get it from your parents' DNA.

definition: all the same kind; uniform throughout
example: The paint must be mixed until it's completely homogeneous without streaks.

definition: the quality of being humble and not thinking you're better than others
example: Despite winning each award, she showed humility by thanking her teammates.

definition: a mixture of two different things
example: The hybrid car runs on both gasoline and electric battery power.

definition: imagined or supposed rather than real
example: The teacher posed a hypothetical question: what if gravity suddenly doubled?

definition: forbidden by law or rules
example: The illicit sale of test answers got three students suspended from school.

definition: to involve deeply or plunge into liquid
example: The best way to learn Spanish is to immerse yourself by living in a Spanish-speaking country.

definition: to weaken or damage something
example: Loud music at concerts can impair your hearing if you don't wear earplugs.

definition: treating all sides equally; unbiased
example: A good judge must be impartial and not favor either the defense or prosecution.

definition: to slow down or block progress
example: Fallen trees on the road will impede traffic until crews clear them away.

definition: to beg desperately for something
example: The children implore their parents to let them stay up for the midnight countdown.

definition: to create something without preparation
example: When she forgot her speech, she had to improvise and speak from memory.

definition: to encourage violent or unlawful behavior
example: The troublemaker tried to incite a food fight by throwing the first roll.

definition: welcoming all regardless of differences
example: The inclusive playground has gear that children of all abilities can enjoy.

definition: a small increase or addition
example: The thermostat adjusts in increments of one degree at a time.

definition: the current holder of an office or position
example: The incumbent mayor is running for re-election against two challengers.

definition: done without careful judgment or selection
example: The indiscriminate use of pesticides harms helpful insects along with harmful ones.

definition: many people or things arriving at once
example: The influx of tourists each summer doubles the town's population.

definition: starting something new or welcoming someone into a group
example: The initiation of the new recycling program begins next Monday.
